description: A mutable memory location that provides interior mutability. Cell allows you to mutate a value even when you only have a shared (immutable) reference. It works by copying the value in and out, so T must implement Copy. Use Cell when you need to mutate data inside an otherwise immutable struct.

methods.name: new
methods.description: Creates a new Cell containing the given value.

methods.name: get
methods.description: Returns a copy of the contained value. Requires T: Copy.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets the contained value. Notice this takes &self, not &mut self, which is the point of interior mutability.

methods.name: replace
methods.description: Replaces the contained value and returns the old value.

methods.name: into_inner
methods.description: Consumes the Cell and returns the wrapped value.
